nodes.name: TT2TDB_Offset
nodes.description: Computes the relativistic time scale offset between Terrestrial Time (TT) and Barycentric Dynamical Time (TDB) in seconds, using a sinusoidal approximation of the periodic correction term derived from Earth's orbital eccentricity.

inputs.name: seconds
inputs.constraints: Elapsed seconds from a reference epoch (typically J2000.0); unbounded real number

outputs.name: offset
outputs.constraints: TDB - TT offset in seconds; typically within ±0.002 s

nodes.type_signature: (seconds: float | array-like of floats) -> float | array-like of floats
nodes.conceptual_summary: PeriodicPhaseOffsetFromElapsedScalar. Maps a monotonically increasing scalar (elapsed time or phase accumulator) to a bounded periodic correction term by evaluating a sinusoidal function whose argument is a linear function of the input. The transform encodes a small-amplitude, low-frequency oscillatory perturbation layered atop an otherwise near-zero baseline, effectively decomposing a continuous parameter into its dominant secular component plus a periodic residual. The output amplitude is strictly bounded by the sinusoid's coefficient, making the transform a smooth, differentiable, and invertible local approximation of a more complex underlying periodic signal.. Applications: Orbital mechanics / astrodynamics: computing the equation-of-center correction — the small periodic difference between mean anomaly and true anomaly for a body on a slightly eccentric Keplerian orbit, where the elapsed angular parameter maps directly to the sinusoidal eccentricity correction term, Signal processing / clock synchronization: modeling the periodic phase jitter introduced by a bounded oscillatory disturbance in a phase-locked loop (PLL), where a carrier's instantaneous phase offset from its nominal linear ramp is approximated by a sinusoidal perturbation parameterized by elapsed sample count, Structural / mechanical engineering: approximating the small periodic strain or displacement residual in a cyclically loaded elastic structure (e.g., a rotating shaft under eccentric load), where cumulative rotation angle is the input and the sinusoidal term captures the once-per-revolution imbalance force component, Geophysics / tidal modeling: computing the first-harmonic approximation of a tidal constituent's instantaneous height deviation from mean sea level, where elapsed seconds since a reference epoch determine the tidal phase and the bounded sinusoidal output represents the dominant periodic sea-surface perturbation
